qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Embedded CGF FTP service uses hardcoded credentials and exposes writable /tmp access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is in the CGF FTP startup path (`internal/cgf/cgf.go`, `pkg/service/init.go`) and concerns hardcoded `admin`/`free5gc` credentials plus `/tmp` file exposure. The advisory is a different root cause in `internal/sbi/api_convergedcharging.go` `Server.RechargePut`, where unchecked parsing of `rechargingInfo` causes out-of-range slice access and a panic-triggerable DoS.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described recharge-path panic root cause. The only qualifying finding is a separate CGF FTP hardcoded-credentials issue.
evidence: The advisory and referenced patch `55af766f321a00afa978e806548c96f8a7d2433e` target `internal/sbi/api_convergedcharging.go` `Server.RechargePut`, adding validation before indexing `strings.Split(rechargingInfo, "_")` and returning HTTP 400 instead of panicking.

The only final qualifying finding cites `internal/cgf/cgf.go:75`, `internal/cgf/cgf.go:91`, `internal/cgf/cgf.go:94`, and `pkg/service/init.go:171`, describing an embedded FTP server with fixed credentials and an exposed `/tmp` root.
No qualifying finding references the recharge endpoint, `RechargePut`, `rechargingInfo` parsing, `ratingGroup` handling, or a slice-bounds panic/DoS condition.
